symptoms: No power at all — no LEDs, no rail activity after connecting wall or battery power.
D9 power-input LED dark even with adapter plugged in.

likely_causes.mechanism: blown SMD input fuse — open circuit, no VIN downstream of fuse
short-to-ground on input bus pulling adapter into current limit
cold joint or pad-lift on barrel-jack solder tabs from mechanical stress

diagnostic_steps.action: Probe VIN at R49 with DMM in DC mode, adapter plugged in, batteries removed
diagnostic_steps.expected: 9–32 V (nominal 24 V); <1 V indicates open input path

diagnostic_steps.action: Continuity / resistance check across F1 in DMM resistance mode
diagnostic_steps.expected: <1 Ω closed; OL = blown fuse

diagnostic_steps.action: Diode-mode VIN to GND on bulk input cap (probe at R49 input side)
diagnostic_steps.expected: >0.3 V or OL; <0.05 V indicates dead short on VIN bus — short-hunt before replacing F1

diagnostic_steps.action: If F1 open and no downstream short found: replace F1 with same-rating SMD fuse, hot air 350°C with flux

symptoms: D11 LED is dark.
System Controller (LPC) does not respond to SYSCTL UART commands.
OLED status display blank with adapter connected.

likely_causes.mechanism: always-on 3.3 V regulator failed open or shorted output, starving U18 of LPC_VCC
downstream short on LPC_VCC rail (leaky MLCC) collapsing U14 output
shorted LPC11U24 supply pin pulling LPC_VCC to GND

diagnostic_steps.action: Probe LPC_VCC at J22 pin 15 with DMM
diagnostic_steps.expected: 3.3 V ± 5%; <0.2 V indicates dead rail

diagnostic_steps.action: Diode-mode LPC_VCC to GND at J22 pin 15
diagnostic_steps.expected: >0.4 V; <0.05 V = short-to-ground on rail, isolate U18 before replacing U14

diagnostic_steps.action: Probe U14 input pin for VIN / pre-regulator supply, then U14 output pin
diagnostic_steps.expected: input present, output 3.3 V; input present + output 0 V = U14 dead

diagnostic_steps.action: If U14 output shorted with U18 lifted: replace U14, hot air 360°C with flux, reflow 30 s

symptoms: LPC UART responds and D11 is lit, but on '1P' power-on command D11 extinguishes immediately.
Nothing further happens; CPU module does not start.

likely_causes.mechanism: short-to-ground on 3.3 V switched rail (leaky MLCC bypass cap) trips LPC overcurrent shutdown
short on 5 V switched rail collapses sequence; D12 never lights
damaged LPC GPIO line to power-rail switch FET (overvoltage / reversed-battery damage)

diagnostic_steps.action: With board off, diode-mode 3V3_PWR_AUX to GND at any decoupling cap on the rail
diagnostic_steps.expected: >0.4 V or OL; <0.05 V = direct short to GND, cap-walk to find leaky MLCC

diagnostic_steps.action: Diode-mode USB_PWR (5 V) to GND at D12 pad
diagnostic_steps.expected: >0.4 V or OL; <0.05 V = 5 V rail shorted

diagnostic_steps.action: Cap-walk along 3V3_PWR_AUX MLCCs with hot air at 250 °C, identify warming cap, lift and re-test
diagnostic_steps.expected: rail short clears once leaky cap removed

diagnostic_steps.action: If no rail short found: probe U18 GPIO output for 3V3_PWR_EN under '1P' command with scope/DMM
diagnostic_steps.expected: logic high (~3.3 V) for at least 100 ms before LPC shuts down

symptoms: SD card boot fails.
System won't read SD card even though LPC and 3.3 V main rail are confirmed live.

likely_causes.mechanism: 1.8 V rail dead — buck regulator output absent or rail shorted to GND
level shifter U28 with shorted VCCB pin pulling VCC_1V8 to GND
level shifter U8 with shorted supply pulling VCC_1V8 to GND

diagnostic_steps.action: Probe VCC_1V8 at C130 with DMM
diagnostic_steps.expected: 1.8 V ± 5%; <0.1 V indicates dead rail

diagnostic_steps.action: Diode-mode C130 to GND
diagnostic_steps.expected: >0.4 V or OL; <0.05 V = rail shorted

diagnostic_steps.action: If rail shorted: lift U28 then U8 in turn; re-diode-mode after each removal to identify the shorted level shifter
diagnostic_steps.expected: diode value rises to >0.4 V once offending IC removed

diagnostic_steps.action: If rail open with no short: locate 1.8 V buck on Power schematic, probe its enable and feedback pins
diagnostic_steps.expected: EN high, FB ≈ internal reference (typ. 0.6–0.8 V) when rail is alive

symptoms: Internal display dark or no video.
USB ports also dead.
External HDMI may or may not work.

likely_causes.mechanism: 1.2 V supply dead — both U9 (TUSB8041) and U10 (SN65DSI86) require 1.2 V to operate
TUSB8041 internal short on VCC_1V2 pin pulling rail to GND
SN65DSI86 internal short on VCC_1V2 pin pulling rail to GND

diagnostic_steps.action: Probe VCC_1V2 at C37 with DMM
diagnostic_steps.expected: 1.2 V ± 5%; <0.1 V indicates dead rail

diagnostic_steps.action: Diode-mode C37 to GND
diagnostic_steps.expected: >0.3 V or OL; <0.05 V = rail shorted

diagnostic_steps.action: If shorted: hot-air-lift U9 first, re-test; then lift U10 if still shorted
diagnostic_steps.expected: rail diode value recovers to >0.3 V after offending IC lifted

diagnostic_steps.action: If VCC_1V2 present but display still dark: probe 3V3_PWR_AUX and USB_PWR at D11/D12, then inspect U10 BGA/QFN joints under microscope
diagnostic_steps.expected: rails present; cold joint visible on U10 → reflow at 360 °C with flux

symptoms: Internal display flickers intermittently or goes blank after reassembly.
HDMI output unaffected.
Display image present but backlight failing intermittently.

likely_causes.mechanism: lifted ZIF pad / fractured contact on 0.5 mm pitch DSI flex connector
cold joint on SN65DSI86 BGA/QFN balls after thermal stress
cold joint or shorted ESD cell on backlight signal line

diagnostic_steps.action: Inspect J24 ZIF pads under stereo microscope at 20–40×
diagnostic_steps.expected: all 30 pads flat, tinned, no liftoff or solder bridging

diagnostic_steps.action: Diode-mode each TPD12S521 pin to GND
diagnostic_steps.expected: >0.3 V or OL; any <0.05 V indicates blown ESD cell — replace TPD12S521

diagnostic_steps.action: Reflow J24 pads with iron + flux at 350 °C; if pad lifted, scrape mask off trace stub and run 38 AWG jumper to connector pin
If J24 mechanically intact: reflow U10 with hot air 360 °C, pre-heat 150 °C, 45 s
diagnostic_steps.expected: display restored after reflow

symptoms: microHDMI connector physically detached from Pocket Reform motherboard.
External display goes black and cannot be reconnected.
Visible mechanical damage at HDMI port.

likely_causes.mechanism: connector pads torn from PCB after repeated plug/unplug cycles, mechanical anchor failed

diagnostic_steps.action: Inspect connector footprint under microscope; identify which signal pads remain on PCB and which are torn
diagnostic_steps.expected: intact pads = reflowable; torn pads = need scraping back to trace and jumpering

diagnostic_steps.action: Continuity test from each surviving signal pad to its first via/cap downstream
diagnostic_steps.expected: <1 Ω; OL = trace damaged inside laminate

diagnostic_steps.action: Clean and re-tin pads, tack new connector with low-temp paste on signal pins, hot air 320 °C
For torn pads: scrape mask off trace stub, run 36–38 AWG jumper from stub to connector pin; reinforce mechanical anchor with two-part epoxy fillet

symptoms: v3.0 motherboard: 5 V or 3.3 V main rails at incorrect voltage.
D11 or D12 indicator dim, oscillating, or off.

likely_causes.mechanism: cold joint on LM62460 thermal pad or feedback pin causing hiccup / output droop
short / leaky output cap on 5 V rail loading regulator into hiccup
short / leaky output cap on 3.3 V rail loading regulator into hiccup

diagnostic_steps.action: Probe USB_PWR at D12 pad and 3V3_PWR_AUX at D11 pad with DMM
diagnostic_steps.expected: 5.0 V and 3.3 V ± 5%; oscillating reading = hiccup mode

diagnostic_steps.action: Diode-mode each rail to GND on the LM62460 output cap
diagnostic_steps.expected: >0.3 V or OL; <0.1 V = leaky cap / shorted load on rail

diagnostic_steps.action: Scope LM62460 feedback pin (FB) for stability
diagnostic_steps.expected: DC level near internal reference (~1 V); ringing or sawtooth = instability

diagnostic_steps.action: Inspect LM62460 solder joints under microscope; reflow with hot air 360 °C and flux if cold joint suspected

symptoms: LPC serial responsive, batteries healthy, '1P' executes (LEDs change), but main LCD stays dark and no CPU UART (Serial 1) output.
Onset after SOM was knocked or after eMMC DIP switch operation.

likely_causes.mechanism: lifted / fractured SODIMM edge pad on motherboard side after mechanical impact
open SODIMM 1.8 V power pin not delivering rail to SOM PMIC
open SODIMM contact on PWRON line — LPC asserts but SOM never sees the signal

diagnostic_steps.action: Remove SOM and inspect SODIMM connector under microscope at 20–40× for lifted pads, bent contacts, oxidation
diagnostic_steps.expected: all contacts clean and coplanar

diagnostic_steps.action: With SOM out, probe SODIMM 3.3 V and 1.8 V power pins on motherboard side under '1P' sequence
diagnostic_steps.expected: 3.3 V and 1.8 V present at motherboard SODIMM pins after LPC enable

diagnostic_steps.action: Continuity from SODIMM SOM_PWRON pin back to U18 GPIO source pin
diagnostic_steps.expected: <5 Ω; OL = broken trace, jumper required

diagnostic_steps.action: Reflow motherboard SODIMM connector pins with iron + flux at 350 °C; if pads lifted, jumper from trace stub to contact

symptoms: Built-in display image present but backlight fails or flickers intermittently.
HDMI works fine.
Toggling backlight intensity in OS sometimes triggers or kills the backlight.

likely_causes.mechanism: shorted ESD cell on backlight PWM line clamping BL_PWM signal
cold joint on backlight PWM trace at eDP FPC connector pin
lifted pad on backlight pin of FPC connector after flex-cable stress

diagnostic_steps.action: Diode-mode each TPD12S521 pin to GND
diagnostic_steps.expected: >0.3 V or OL on all pins; <0.05 V on any pin = blown ESD cell

diagnostic_steps.action: Probe BL_PWM at eDP FPC connector pin with scope while toggling OS backlight
diagnostic_steps.expected: PWM waveform 0–3.3 V at brightness-dependent duty cycle

diagnostic_steps.action: Inspect eDP FPC connector pads and TPD12S521 joints under microscope
diagnostic_steps.expected: no lifted pads, no cold joints; reflow at 350 °C if cold joint visible

diagnostic_steps.action: If TPD12S521 has shorted pin: replace IC with hot air 360 °C, pre-heat 130 °C, flux + low-temp paste
